english: rna-spábs (rna spabs) C. ear-wax; Lexx. also rṅul- (rngul-) (or rdul-? (rdul-?)) gyi spabs (gyi spabs) w.e.
spar-k'a brgyad (spar kha brgyad) the pah kwah, or eight diagrams of Chinese science, ☰☱ etc.

I. sbst., also spar-mo (spar mo) (Ld. *wár-mo*, acc. to the spelling sbar-mo (sbar mo)) 1. the grasping hand, paw, claw, spráṅ-poi spár-mor spa-dbyúg sprad (sprang po'i spar mor spa dbyug sprad) he puts the staff into the beggar's grasp (hand) Lexx.; *wár-mo gyáb-ce* (war mo gyab ce) W., spár-mos °brád-pa (spar mos brad pa) to clutch, to scratch; spár-mos °débs-pa (spar mos debs pa) Cs., spar byéd-pa (spar byed pa) Sch., to seize with the hand, the paw, or the claws; ydoṅ tams-cád spar-śád rgyáb-pa (gdong thams cad spar shad rgyab pa) Pth. to scratch the whole face ('combing it with the claws'); *sbar-ju* (sbar ju) C. rail, for taking hold of; spár-mo °byéd-pa, bsdám-pa (spar mo byed pa, bsdam pa) to open, to close the hand Cs. - 2. as a measure: as much as may be grasped with the hand, a handful (of wood, grass, earth etc.), *(s)pár-ra yaṅ* ((s)par ra yang) one handful, (s)pár-ra gaṅ do ((s)par ra gang do) two handfuls etc.; spar-tsád lṅa-brgyá (spar tshad lnga brgya) 500 handfuls S.g.; sa spar-gáṅ (sa spar gang) Mil. a handful of earth. II. vb. v. spór-ba (spor ba).
a low-growing shrub of very hard wood Mil. nt.
Spiti, the valley, situated to the west of Lahul, watered by the Spiti river, belonging to the British Punjab, and inhabited by a race of pure Tibetans.

1. hair ('pilus', cf. skra (skra)), lús-kyi (lus kyi) of the body in general, Lex.; mgó-spu, k'á- or ydóṅ-spu, mcán-spu, °doms-spu (mgo spu, kha or gdong spu, mchan spu, doms spu) (or spu-ṅán (spu ngan) Cs.), bráṅ-spu (brang spu), hair of the head, the beard, arm-pits, lower-parts, chest; bá-spu (ba spu) the little hairs of the skin, frq.; rtá-spu (rta spu) horse-hair; spu p'yí or ytog (spu phyi or gtog) the hair is plucked out Lex., byí (byi) falls off Dzl., yźob byed (gzhob byed) is singed off Sch., ldaṅ, laṅ (ldang, lang) Dzl., lóṅ-yyo (long g-yo) Mil., the hair bristles, stands on end: spu ziṅ byed (spu zing byed) B., brtse (brtse) Sch., *se-ziṅ* (se zing) W., a shuddering of fear comes over (me, him etc.); tams-cád spu-ziṅ byéd-ciṅ (thams cad spu zing byed cing) Pth.; byad spus keṅs-pa (byad spus kengs pa) with a face all hairy Glr.; spui k'uṅ-bu (spu'i khung bu) passage of perspiration, pore Dzl.; spu nyág-ma tsám-gyi gyód-pai sems (spu nyag ma tsam gyi gyod pa'i sems) repentance as much as one single little hair Dzl. - 2. feather, byá-spu rluṅ-giś k'yer-ba (bya spu rlung gis khyer ba) a down (feather) blown off; feathers, plumage. Comp. spú-k'a (spu kha) colour of horses and other hairy animals. - spú-gri (spu gri) 1. razor; also allegorically, as a title of books. 2. knife C. - spú-can (spu can) hairy. - spu-cím (spu cim) (?) false hair Sch. - spú-ja (spu ja) v ja (ja). - spú-byi nág-po, spu-nág (spu byi nag po, spu nag) also pú-byi (pu byi), sable (furred animal) Sch. - spú-ma (spu ma) hairy, carded (cloth). - spu-méd (spu med) hairless. - spu-ytsaṅ-ma (spu gtsang ma) v. spus (spus). - spu-hrúg (spu hrug) short-haired Sch.
Lexx. n. of a precious stone Cs.
heap, col. also for púṅ-po (pung po); spúṅ-ba (spung ba) pf. and imp. -spuṅs (-spungs), to heap, accumulate, pile up (coals etc.); rin-cén spuṅs-pa (rin chen spungs pa) a heap of precious stones Glr.
to decorate; rgyán-gyis (rgyan gyis) Lex. (cf. spus (spus)).

1. children of the same parents, brothers, sisters, k'ó-mo-cag spun ynyis (kho mo cag spun gnyis) we (his) two sisters Dzl. 240, 17; ṅed spun ysum (nged spun gsum) we three brothers Glr.; k'yed bú-mo spun lṅa-po (khyed bu mo spun lnga po) you five sisters Mil.; pleon. bu spun ysum (bu spun gsum) Tar.; spun yźán-rnams (spun gzhan rnams) his other (six elder) brothers Tar.; spun-ycés (spun gces) dear brother! Chr. P. - pá-spun (pa spun), brothers and sisters of the same father; má-spun (ma spun) of the same mother; spún-zla, (s)pun-da (spun zla, (s)pun da), or -la (-la) 1. = spun (spun); 2. in C. it is said to be used also for attorney, advocate; spún-ma (spun ma) sister, as a more particular designation of the sex. - 2. in a wider sense: cousins, brothers- or sisters-in-law; grógs-spun (grogs spun) mate, comrade; c'ós-spun (chos spun) a brother of a religious order; pá-spun, p'ás-spun (pa spun, phas spun), several neighbours or inhabitants of a village, that have a common Lha, and thus have become *rus-pa cig-cig* (rus pa cig cig), members of the same family; this common tie entails on them the duty,
